description: 71M, CKD 4 (eGFR 22), albuminuria A2 (ACR 85 mg/g), T2DM (16-year duration), HTN. Advanced CKD near the eGFR 20 SGLT2 threshold and eGFR 15 statin/dialysis boundary.

actions.label: Initiate SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin or dapagliflozin)
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1A: CKD + T2DM with eGFR 22 (≥20 threshold met, just above the contraindication cutoff). SGLT2i slows CKD progression; critical at G4 where trajectory toward dialysis is a near-term concern. Note: once initiated, can continue even if eGFR drops below 20.

actions.label: Initiate ACE inhibitor or ARB
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: ACR 85 mg/g (A2) with diabetes and hypertension. RAS blockade reduces proteinuria and slows CKD progression. BP 142/86 is uncontrolled; ACEi/ARB provides dual benefit of nephroprotection and BP reduction. Monitor potassium closely at eGFR 22 — hyperkalemia risk is elevated.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1A: age 71, eGFR 22 (G4, ≥15 not-on-dialysis proxy met). Moderate-intensity only — high-intensity contraindicated in advanced CKD. Statin clearance is significantly reduced at G4; start low and monitor for myopathy.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and ACR every 1-3 months
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: G4-A2 is 'very high risk' on the KDIGO heatmap. eGFR trajectory monitoring is critical at G4 for dialysis planning. Also monitor for medication-related eGFR dips after SGLT2i and ACEi/ARB initiation.

actions.label: Nephrology referral for advanced CKD management
actions.rationale: eGFR 22 (G4) warrants nephrology co-management per KDIGO. Pre-dialysis education, vascular access planning, and transplant evaluation should begin. Multiple new medications being initiated require coordinated management.

contraindications.label: High-intensity statin therapy
contraindications.rationale: eGFR 22 (G4) significantly alters statin pharmacokinetics. High-intensity carries unacceptable myopathy risk at this level of renal impairment.

contraindications.label: Combined ACEi + ARB
contraindications.rationale: Dual RAS blockade increases hyperkalemia risk, which is already elevated at eGFR 22. Use one agent only.

contraindications.label: Conservative management without pharmacotherapy
contraindications.rationale: At G4 with T2DM and albuminuria, the trajectory toward kidney failure is measurable. Evidence-based interventions (SGLT2i, RAS blockade, statin) have clear benefit in slowing progression. Deferral risks accelerated decline.
